Passengers should not be required to repeatedly travel to the airport to retrieve baggage caused by airline handling errors. Proper coordination, transparency, and customer care are expected. I request: • Immediate confirmation of final baggage routing • Delivery to my next destination (Fes) as agreed • Reimbursement for essential purchases made due to the delay This experience has significantly affected my trip and confidence in your airline.

Name: Marcus D
Comments: Bad service Bad airline Bad air hostess You have to know something mfk we pay our fkn money

Name: sandranavarro00
Comments: I just don't have the stregnth to write anymore, so I have to summarize: terrible experience, never again

Name: Isaac Enmanuel V
Comments: I am writing this review after an extremely frustrating experience with Royal Air Maroc. My checked baggage was delayed during an international connection and I have now spent almost 48 hours in Morocco without my suitcase, which contained all my clothing, personal belongings, toiletries, and medically necessary items. What is most unacceptable is not just the delay, but the total lack of coordination and accountability. I received no proactive communication, no tracking information, no delivery timeline, and no clear answers despite multiple calls and emails. At one point, customer service informed me that my baggage had been handed over to a third-party delivery company, provided only a generic Gmail address, and then attempted to shift responsibility entirely to that third party — despite the airline remaining fully responsible until delivery. This situation caused serious personal and professional disruption, including the inability to attend important meetings properly prepared. Customer service responses were slow, vague, and inconsistent, requiring constant follow-ups from my side just to obtain basic information. While the flight itself may get you from point A to point B, Royal Air Maroc’s post-flight service and baggage management are deeply unreliable. If anything goes wrong, be prepared to fight for updates, accountability, and resolution. I strongly advise travelers — especially those with connections, time-sensitive commitments, or essential items in checked luggage — to think very carefully before choosing this airline.

Name: Rich M
Comments: I am very disappointed with my recent experience with Royal Air Maroc. The flight was delayed by 3 hours and 30 minutes, with no clear explanation, no apology, and no form of compensation offered to passengers. Communication during the delay was almost nonexistent, which made the situation even more frustrating. On board, the overall service quality was poor. The cabin crew appeared unprofessional and disengaged, and the level of English from the senior cabin crew was extremely weak, to the point where basic communication was difficult. This is not acceptable for an international airline. More concerning was the lack of basic safety procedures during landing. No crew member checked whether seats were upright or if passengers’ seat belts were properly fastened. This is a fundamental safety responsibility, and ignoring it is simply unacceptable. Between the long delay, the absence of customer care, poor service standards, and safety oversights, this experience fell far below what passengers should expect from a national airline.

Name: Shelley M
Comments: First and last time using this carrier. Food was TERRIBLE. Cabin crew unfriendly, no wine for dinner on the return flight and finally, we paid extra for bulk head seats but they moved us to another row not having the extra leg room. It was paid on my credit card and will dispute. I'd gladly paid extra to fly on another carrier when I go back to visit Morocco

Name: Escape26496536849
Comments: My baggage was delayed for several days after arrival in Dakar (23 Jan 2026). I immediately filed a report and later submitted a compensation claim with receipts and reference number. Despite multiple follow-ups, Royal Air Maroc has completely ignored my emails. The lack of response and accountability is extremely disappointing. I would not recommend this airline based on my experience.

Name: EdgeoftheCay
Comments: RAM is an complete embarrassment to the country of Morocco! Worst international flight experience of all time: October 10th, 2025 – AT219 (Dulles International IAD to Casablanca CMN) October 24th, 2025 – AT218 (Casablanca CMN to Dulles International IAD) **Only 3.5 weeks before our trip, RAM informed us that our return flight AT218 on 10/22 had been cancelled and moved 2 DAYS LATER to 10/24 (should we wish to accept it). The new return date required us to unravel and rebook our on-ground logistics and lodging, resulting in $3,200 in additional expense. **RAM changed the aircraft on both the departure and return flights from 787-9s to 787-8s, which changed the seating configurations. We discovered that our Business Class seats had been arbitrarily reassigned and we were now seated side-by-side with total strangers across the cabin from each other. We had to call RAM customer service to fix the situation and move our seats back together. **The night before the return flight, RAM sent an email that our flight had been delayed by more than six hours. While en route to the airport, we got another email informing us that our flight had changed again and was now scheduled to depart more than seven and a half hours past the original departure time. **On the departure flight from IAD to CMN - the electronic keypad on my brother's seat glitched and his seat was unable to go flat. Return flight from CMN to IAD, the electronic keypads on BOTH our seats (1D and IF) stopped working. 1D was unable to lay flat and 1F got stuck completely in the flat position. Although flight attendants tried to reset the power boxes – it took two flight attendants to manually wrestle the seats back into position. **RAM also sent multiple emails to us stating, ‘New Entry Requirement for Morocco’ with our booking numbers referenced - - only to discover the entry requirements had NOTHING to do with our flights or our originating countries and were only relevant for passport holders from Algeria, Burkina Faso, Cape Verde, Gabon, Niger, Senegal, Togo and Tunisia going to CAN 2025 We submitted a claim on 10/31 outlining all the issues and requesting that only 1/2 our ticked cost be refunded to us. RAM never responded, so we had to call 4 times over 2.5 months to get an update on our claim. They finally responded to us on 1/20/26: "We regret for not being able to respond favorably to your request as our regulations do not allow it. We assure you that the satisfaction of our customers is at the heart of our ambitions and our mission, and ask you to please accept the expression of our highest consideration. We look forward to having the pleasure of welcoming you once again aboard Royal Air Maroc flights." Welcome us onboard again? Never. While the actual flight attendants and pilots were competent and professional, DO NOT PUT YOURSELF THROUGH THE STRESS OF FLYING ROYAL AIR MAROC - they are a grossly incompetent and unreliable airline that doesn't care at all about the customer experience.

Name: Interior Design... b
Comments: The worst experience with an airline. Upon my arrival in egypt they couldn't find out luggage, we had 5 luggages as we are a family of 5. I have three kids and it was so unpleasant to arrive with non of our stuff. Once it was delivered two of the baggaes were damaged. I filed a complaint as soon as I was back from my vacation with an automatic reply that someone will get back to me. I called numerous times and they said someone will take care of my file. They finally reached out and sked me to send them proof. Which I already had but once again I sent everything they asked for. Months with no one answering and I continued to send follow up emails. In december someone answered me saying that the claim was such as long time ago and it is no longer valid. That made me furious I was like I did the claim in august 2025 not even 2 weeks after the issue and you answer 4 months after saying its not valid. THIS IS BY FAR THE WORST AIRLINE EVER. THEY HAVE NO RESPECT FOR HUMANITY AND THEREFORE I WILL HAVE TO TAKE LEGAL ACTION.

Name: Adam
Comments: I will never fly RAM again. Had an 8h40m transit in Casablanca, flying in and out on Royal Air Maroc. They offer a free lounge and food vouchers for long transits, but because I bought the tickets on the Qatar Airways website they refused me them. They still get the money whichever website I used. Just an awful policy that has lost them any future custom from me. Casablanca is also terrible airport to transit by the way. Just avoid.

Name: Beverley H
Comments: The flight was delayed by 24 hrs causing me to miss a funeral that I booked the flight to attend. Compensation offered and has taken 4 months to yet be paid out. Customer Service is ridiculous still waiting for Compensation to be paid. Worse airline I've ever travelled on, would never recommend to anyone. Absolutely awful 😖.

Name: Scenic17500620325
Comments: I had a direct flight from JFK to Morocco. I checked my bags 3 hours before the flight. They lost my luggage. Customer service was useless and provided absolutely no help. Not only did my luggage have the tags that the airport put on them but it also had a very prominent personal tag I put on my bag with my email address and cell telephone number.

Name: Kehinde A
Comments: Damaged bag, missing contents in the bag. No response after reporting till date Royal Air Maroc | Your ticket N° :358701400308

Name: RANIA E
Comments: Worst experience. Traveled with a big group of friends. They lost our luggage on both flights. Till date since December 21st the luggage is lost. No compensation, we can’t allocate them Definitely not recommended

Name: Rachel J
Comments: The airplane interior was dirty and outdated. The 6-hour CAI to CMN flight had no entertainment screen, and my seat did not recline. I had no movie to watch, and because I had to sit upright, I could not sleep the entire time. The 8.5-hour CMN to IAD flight did have an entertainment screen, but the video froze every five minutes or so. I had to stop and resume it over and over again. I have never seen flight attendants as lazy as they were. They served one cup of water, tea/coffee, and the meal all at once, and then stayed in the back for the rest of the flight. They did not reappear until landing. Drinks were never served separately before or after that. Unlike other airlines, they did not collect trash from passengers. The inside of the plane was a complete mess, with trash scattered throughout. The meal was terrible. Truly terrible. It looked like a meal designed for a child who only eats carbohydrates. I have never seen such a small portion of protein. There were no fresh vegetables or fruit at all, and the meal was more than 80% carbohydrates. The tray was filled with different kinds of tasteless, cheap bread. I was hungry and thirsty throughout the flight. For reference, I don’t eat much. I cannot even finish a Big Mac meal, and a 6-inch Subway sandwich is enough for me. I often saw passengers going to the flight attendants’ rest area to ask for water. If this were a budget airline, I could understand it a little. But they charge more than $2,000 for a round-trip economy ticket on my route, and the service was not even up to the budget-airline standards. It was absolutely not worth the money. I flew with this airline four times, and my experience was consistently the same. I am a frequent flyer and travel often. I have flown with nearly 30 different airlines so far, and this was the worst airline and service I have ever experienced.

Name: Jose Manuel D
Comments: Avoid to travel to Mauritania with Royal Air Maroc (RAM). This company does not respect guidelines for hand luggage. The manager of RAM in Nouackchott airport forces to check in hand luggage unless you pay him a bribe. Unbelievable!!!

Name: Christine
Comments: Flight AT209 December 26/25 Montreal to Malaga - change in Casablanca. Flight delayed into Casablanca. Luggage was tagged with Short connection bag tagged and luggage did not get transferred on fight in Casablanca to Malaga Reported missing upon landing Dec 27/25 in Malaga - flight AT988 Received call Dec 28/25 from Royal Air Maroc - then nothing rest of my vacation. I told them my air tag stated the suitcase was in Terminal 1 in Casablanca. I attempted to call Royal Air Maroc x3 during my vacation and was unable to get through to a live agent. Unable to submit a complaint through their website as flight ticket number was incorrect when I copied it direct. Returned home January 2/26 - checked with Aviapartner in Malaga about status of my luggage. The agent in Malaga said Air Maroc "don't care" and to check when i get to Casablanca. The flight of course was delayed into Casablanca and I attempted to checked with an agent in Casablanca who said she didn't understand English and just shrugged. Since being home I have been unsuccessful in getting any response. I agree with sentiments on other’s on Trip Advisor, avoid this air line and pay the extra to fly on another airline.

Name: Melanie L
Comments: Awful experience. RAM cancelled the flight 30mins before departure. No reason given and no alternative flight offered. Still waiting for a refund.

Name: mouna m
Comments: It was the worst trip ever I had a flight from Malaga to Casablanca and than to Doha Qatar, and due to a flight light from their side I didn’t reached my flight and I had the bad ever customer service starting from their airlines and they told me to change my flight for tomorrow and the bad think which makes the situation worst they send me to a place called oasis where the lady in the reception where so rude and told me that you have to seat on the chair till 9 pm means I have to wait 6 hours on the chair till they will see if they can find a bed for me or not and when I was asking how like this this is not my mistake she told me if you you are not ok call the police !!! at that time I went to an hotel and I pay the full cost from my pocket , I will never fly with them again and I will never recommend any one to fly with them The worst ever airlines and the people who’s working the airport worst than them for the way how to talk that we can not do anything for you !? Is it my mistake to travel with your airlines paying my hotel and my food and everything from my pocket May god take a revenge from all of you

Name: MitchNewman650
Comments: We flew comfortably on business from JFK to Casablanca. Food and service were good. A little better than airlines like United or American. Upon landing we discovered we lost a bag. That's when the incompetent customer service began. First clue, the Casablanca baggage claim area is FILLED with unclaimed bags sequestered in groups. Second clue, the number they give you to call after filling out your paperwork doesn't have the country code. Clue three, it doesn't work even if you do have a country code. (We started calling the US number.) We lost our bag on a Tuesday, on Thursday they found it. They never called us but we called every night. We told them it was too late to send it to Tangier but Fes would work. We were told they would call. Next night, no call, so we called. We learned nothing had happened. The bag was still in Casablanca and there was no schedule to send it, Fes. Next night, still no word from Royal Moroccan Air (RAM). We called again from Fes. This time the line no answer for 45 minutes. I sent RAM an email, searched for a way to escalate the issue but could not find a number.. I found a form to fill out on line. It asked for every possible piece of information as if record locator wasn't enough. Every time I tried to submit it, it said something was incorrect and blocked entering the complaint. Next evening, I called again. RAM answered. Still nothing. The bag was in Casablanca and the rep knew that we had requested it to Fes. We were now leaving Fes so I requested it go to Marrakesh. We never had one communication initiated by RAM and I asked to speak to a supervisor. They told me that they didn't have a supervisor. I asked who was in charge of luggage and they told the personnel on the airport. I asked for their number; they didn't have it. How about someone in charge of them? Nope. Who can I speak to complain to? No one. I had to fill out the complaint on line. I read all the info that was required on the form and the rep discovered that the ticket number was incorrect. It was an American Airlines ticket number not a RAM ticket number. It was, however, the ticket number on my ticket. They used another number and gave it to me. That's RAM's system. Ask for a number you can't possibly if you bought your ticket through a sister airline. This screens you from filing a complaint. It's probably not intentional but it does cut down on the complaints. I put in the new number I was give and voila: still didn't work. I finally called the Advantage Desk at American Airlines and they got to work on it. What will happen next we don't know. Nothing Royal is expected from the King's airline. I hope his ears are burning. I may write Mohammed VI, King of Morocco next to tell him he should be embarrassed.

Name: TheFly22
Comments: Terrible customer service. From baggage check-in to boarding your flight, its all subpar compared to many other airlines I've flown with. Add constant delays with no communication on when or why, along with a lost bag for our entire trio, and you can guess what my review rating would be.

Name: Simone C
Comments: Absolutely horrible experience. Stuck on the plane for 3 unnecessary hours at the departure airport because "we are experiencing issues with loading luggage". Arrived in Casablanca with more than 3 hours delay to find out that my baggage (along with many others) had remained in Manchester. The lost and found desk completely useless and arrogant "you expect me to know when the next flight from Manchester will be? Check online". Here for a work trip and without the necessary clothing and documents. And in a complete limbo as to when my baggage will be with me. Ah and of course no delivery to my hotel. I have to go to the airport myself...if it will ever arrive.

Name: Moe T
Comments: I wouldn’t say that RAM is the worst ever but it has its fair share of issues and flaws. I missed my connecting flight in Casablanca, so I decided to take the train from Casa to Marrakech. The nightmare started n Marrakech when I tried to locate my luggage. I was told to go back to Casa to find my luggage. The nightmare continued when I found out that RAM cancelled my return ticket from Marrakech to NYC. I was forced to purchase a brand new one way ticket to NYC for $ 941. My initial round trips ticket was for $ 1,100. I believe what RAM does is not fair to passengers. RAM agents have o answer to why the cancellation took place. The only answer they give is “ The system does the cancellation not us”. The system is man made, it did not come from the sky. When back in NY, I will definitely be contacting the airline so that my lawyer can start a legal procedure. Punishing passengers this way is not going to sit well with so many of us that went through this horror of experience 👎

Name: lucky
Comments: Horrible experience with Royal Air Maroc. Their customer service is extremely poor; they ignored all my emails about lost baggage, which they also mishandled on a previous flight. On my latest trip, they changed my seat at the gate without notice, leaving me stuck on an overnight flight. Flights were late, staff unhelpful, and overall it was a stressful and frustrating experience. I would not recommend flying with them.

Name: Masha A
Comments: The worse airline on the planet! Whether u fly economy or business makes no difference. Your luggage can be equally lost. It’s been 10 days and my luggage contains insulin injections that need to be refrigerated! Medicine costs a fortune and moreover it is impossible to obtain in the country where I’m at the moment. The airline isn’t responding to foreign luggage liaisons, no emails, no calls, only Instagram social media works. I invite all passengers who have suffered at the hands of this mishandling airline to write to media outlets and join us in search of my missing luggage!

Name: Yiga U
Comments: I have a 15 hours delay in Casanblanca, without any complimentary hotel. Just sat for 14 hours. Then the flight was delayed extra 40 mins. Got to JFK New York and didn't see my check-in luggage. It's been two days now.

Name: Jenefer k
Comments: Never ever again will I use air Morocco, worst airline ever I wished I read the reviews before booking. Spent £3500 for business class and you will think your luggage will be a priority I guess not. Travelled for a child’s birthday and my best friend wedding had all the outfits including the wedding dress that was bought in the uk, only for the suitcase to arrive 4 days after and completely ruined what was supposed to be a magical moment. I will not recommend it at all!!!

Name: Anders P
Comments: Banjul (Gambia) Casablanca on 24th Dec. 2025. This was 1st leg on a lengthy journey across Africa. RAM in Banjul could not issue all onward Boarding Passes with OW-partners, but it was later amended in Casablanca. Aircraft was surprisingly upgraded from a narrowbody to a Dreamliner B787-800, filled by only 20+ persons. Took a seat in the middle, surrounded by loads of empty seats. The catering was hasty and very superficial, only a single cold sandwich was served, wine given upon request, though. I felt comfortable with all the space and IFE. Got no extra sandwich despite requesting it but was offered extra local red wine instead - without requesting it. On this 3-hour red-eye flight the staff was hardly seen and the galleys unstaffed. Hear-sets provided for IFE, which was good. However, no pillows or blankets could be provided and cabin was chilly. In Casablanca, RAM kept their website promises of providing a smooth hotel stop-over including meals and transport for passengers with 6+ hours transit. Immigration hurdle was to queue for more than ½ hour. I was put up in a single room in the well-functioning 3 star Relax hotel (owned by the airline). Forre breakfast they provided fresh baked bread, served individually at tables. Got also a nice lunch within my 10 hours’ stay. I felt pampered and respected. RAM provides 2 x 23 kg luggage for luggage checked to final destination, in this case to Rwanda. 2 suitcases made it well, though a handle had gone missing. 2nd time with RAM, ticket not expensive, cooperation with Oneworld and Qatar works. Miles given. Recommend.

Name: Kemi O
Comments: **Avoid at All Costs - Lost Luggage with Zero Support** I'm writing this on day 6 without my luggage after flying with Royal Air Maroc on December 19th. My bag has been missing since I landed, and I still have absolutely no idea where it is or when (or if) I'll ever see it again. This was supposed to be my Christmas break - something I looked forward to all year. Instead, I've spent the entire week dealing with customer service that has no answers, no updates, and seemingly no ability to locate my belongings. Every call yields the same response: they don't know where my bag is. I now have only 4 days left of my trip, none of my belongings, and zero confidence I'll be reunited with my luggage. No clothes, and no personal items. The complete lack of communication and follow-through from Royal Air Maroc has been appalling. My advice: pay the extra money and fly with a more reputable, trustworthy airline. The small savings are absolutely not worth the risk of having your entire trip ruined with no support whatsoever when things go wrong. One star is generous. I would give zero if I could.

Name: Sightsee18282810916
Comments: Very bad company lost luggage and they don’t care. Also, you have to take 2 buses from airport terminal to the plane and their planes are outdated. I’d never fly with them again, stay away.

Name: Andrea F
Comments: Horrible experience. We missed the flight because when we arrived at the gate there was no one even though on the screen it eas written "embarking ". We waitited for someone to show up, no one call us and suddenly the gate closed. You left us without any notice, and we had to buy a new ticket and spend an extra night in Marocco because there were no more flights to Italy. What a shame, avoid this company!

Name: Jaan-Ts
Comments: Absolute garbage that makes the likes of RyanAir and EasyJet look like luxury airlines. I do not know HOW this sad excuse of an airline got accepted into OneWorld, but it should definitely not be a part of that alliance. A truly appalling combination of : 1. The airline being incompetent beyond belief - flights rescheduled, no alternatives provided, calls unanswered, offices closed, emails ignored for 10 days and counting. The only lounge in Marrakesh was closed, making me sit in a small dirty room that did not even have water provided. The business / first lounge in Casablanca was open, but it was absolutely pathetic, felt like a bad school cafeteria with some very basic snacks that were completely inedible, some very bad tasting wine and their local tea, which was the only thing I could drink. Left hungry and very angry. 2. Very bad attitude of all staff members, who did not even pretend to care about the issues covered above. If you are planning to use this airline / do not do it. Do not be fooled by the word “Royal” or the OneWorld alliance logo like I did, this garbage airline should not exist.

Name: VIAJE EN MARRUECOS
Comments: Extremely poor service. Very disappointing experience. I took my flight at 7:00 p.m. with a one-hour layover in Casablanca, scheduled to arrive in Marrakech at 10:30 p.m. Instead, the delay turned it into a five-hour wait, and we didn’t arrive until 3:30 a.m., completely exhausted.

Name: caleb p
Comments: My experience with RAM (Royal Air Maroc) was beyond UNACCEPTABLE . At Casablanca, I was denied boarding without any valid reason while the gate was still open. The airline had OVERBOOKED the flight and failed to provide any prior communication. As a result, I was forced to wait 12 hours OVERNIGHT at the airport with no food, no water, and no accommodation. FAMILIES with CHILDREN were left in the same appalling conditions. To make matters worse, RAM (Royal Air Maroc) then charged me ADDITIONAL FEES to get on the next flight. When I finally boarded, my LUGGUAGE did not arrive at the destination. The entire ordeal was marked by INCOMPETENCE, UNPROFESSIONALISM, and discriminatory behavior (RACISTE). Royal Air Maroc demonstrated a complete disregard for basic customer care and human decency. I would strongly advise anyone to avoid this airline at all costs.

Name: clint P
Comments: Yea avoid at all cost. 2 hrs before the flight they delay for 13hrs. This is just a short flight from fez to casablanca so not understanding the crazy 13 hrs delay. Anyhow we had connections out of casablanca in the afternoon same day and now we had to pay $300 plus in euro to get a driver to take us last minute. I called the airline. They refuse to refund and say we can only reschedule. What a joke and the guy on the phone is ridiculous dumb. Should I reschedule a flight with a company i will never use again and leaving this place never to come back. Royal air is the reason for not returning.

Name: Andri Mateo
Comments: Terrible airline we waited in Agadir airport for 5 hours we were going to Casablanca and we get the information 19:45 when our flight was scheduled 19:15 that the flight would be cancelled and the staff there didn’t know anything

Name: Anthony A
Comments: The plane do not have charging port. This plane should be scrapped. Its old fashioned. It has ONE TOILET for the economy class. This is health and safety issue and IATA will be notified to address this with the company. Its not acceptable at all.

Name: LangRen
Comments: Non working charging port, No sound or partial sound coming to the headphone, Movies and music randomly skipped back to the beginning, Torn pocket, No response when the button to call for attendant was pushed Refused to collect food tray and asked passenger to put tray on the floor Received text from airline to check in online when checking in online returned an error message and asked passenger to check in at the airport in person. Flight attendant rude and condescending to passengers unable to speak either English and French Refused to let passengers use restroom in plane when boarding was still ongoing

Name: Margreet V
Comments: Royal Air Maroc caused us to miss our connecting flight due to their own handling failures. We were not allowed to disembark in time, the first bus to the terminal left without us, and we were delayed again by long queues at passport control. None of these issues were within our control. Despite this, the airline refuses to take responsibility and denied our claim. The only compensation offered was a refund of airport taxes. After this experience, we will never fly with Royal Air Maroc again.

Name: Haddy N
Comments: I spent a long time away from my family and planned to see them all at my brother’s wedding however royal flop maroc had other plans and left me stranded in Casablanca when I was supposed to be at my brothers wedding. The only reason I had accommodation was because of my UK passport I feel for all with west african passports they just hateee to see you coming.

Name: Sto N
Comments: African stop traveling with Air Morocco if you get stock in Morocco you are on your own you will not get help and eveone in Morocco is rude

Name: Howard V
Comments: Baggage did not arrive, after waiting an hour after arrival at 1.30 am. On arrival I was advised that 'baggage on Royal Air Marco not making it to Banjul' was a frequent occurance. There was a large crowd of similarly frustrated passengers outside the baggage services office. To add insult to injury it would have taken an hour or two to do the paperwork at the baggage services desk. As it was 2.30 am I did not wait. Even though the airline must be aware of the acute problem, no arrangements made to assist passengers with extra ground staff. Attempts to get answers online proved fruitless. I will avoid RAM next time.

Name: Victor O
Comments: Worst experience, canceled flight no communication. They are absolutely ridiculous. They don’t even care about your schedule. They got me frustrated.

Name: Alexander A
Comments: Damage Luggage submitted a claim and it was not addressed properly to replace the suitcase. Ignored my numerous emails to replace the suitcase and pay for a replacement.

Name: Mohamed E
Comments: I have used more than a dozen airlines and this airline is by far the worst, their customer service don’t know nothing about customer service, had a lot of issues with them but the recent one is that I requested to pay for an extra bag two days before the trip and their customer service rep didn’t put in the request after he confirmed with me, so I ended up paying a lot more extra because the request was completed the day of the flight, even though it was their rep fault and they knew that from The call records but they still said we can’t do anything about it!

Name: George A
Comments: Watest airline ever do not fly on it. I had very terrible experience with their customer service. I had return ticket. I missed the first leg and then they canceled the whole ticket so I couldn't return back and they even didn't want to issue a refund

Name: Raluca M
Comments: I was honestly a bit reluctant about flying with Royal Air Maroc because I had read quite a few negative reviews beforehand. But after the trip, I believe it’s only fair to share my own experience, which was very positive. I traveled Nantes–Casablanca–Banjul and back, and all four flights were on time - one even left ahead of schedule (the Banjul–Casablanca flight was supposed to leave at 2:30 AM, but by 2:15 we were already leaving the gate in Banjul). My luggage arrived without any problems, which was something I had worried about after reading prior reviews. The cabin crew did great on all flights. I also liked the details onboard - seats, cups and sugar packets decorated with Moroccan designs, and the food boxes promoting the upcoming African Cup of Nations, which will be held in Morocco. Details, but they made the experience more thoughtful. The planes themselves were quite old, but I didn’t mind at all. In fact, they were more comfortable than some newer aircraft I’ve flown on, with leather seats and extra legroom. Overall, what a good experience with RAM!

Name: Navin A
Comments: One of the worst airlines we've flown in. The aircrafts are old, seats are uncomfortable, the staff on-board are obnoxious and overall our vacation travel was the worst experience ever. Will never fly in these lesser known airlines again.

Name: Trip33578716650
Comments: Clearly these people do not think they need to apologize for cancelling a flight. The personnel flies of the handle when you ask them for more information (specially if you are a colored person) and they leave you to fend for yourself with immigration services to retrieve your luggage. There was no one at the RAM booth when we got out of the international zone. They mad eus wait for an hour without a word then got us in a bus and dropped us off at a seedy hotel god knows where in Tunis. Moreover, we were forced (dozens of complete strangers) to share rooms because “the venue is at full capacity” without regard to our need for privacy and, apparently, personal welfare. It’s 1 AM the next day and I still don’t know when (or even if) is the pickup, tomorrow… At this point, I’m even ashamed to be of Moroccan descent. This company is supposed to embody the openheartedness of a people, but all it does is embody its prejudice. I’m never booking a flight with them again (if I make it home, that is).

Name: Dilawar k
Comments: Please avoid this crappy airline at all costs. This lost our luggage twice firstly going to Paris. It came on next flight. On the way back it took them 1 week to deliver it. Horrible old air planes. All 4 flights were delayed

Name: Stephen L
Comments: Flew to Conakry Guinea on Royal Air Maroc (RAM) from London Heathrow via Casablanca on 11th November 2025. They sell the flights with a 50 min transit time. Which is ludicrous. Surprisingly the suitcase never made the connecting flight to Casablanca and it was 3 days before the bag arrived. And when it did the locked North Face 80L Roll bag was soaking wet inside and out. It was if they had put a hosepipe into the bag.

Name: J B
Comments: Very non friendly service at the luggage check in/ they never accept your online pass The partner airline cancelled on me and they would not take responsibility And now I have to stay over night at the New York airport

Name: Favour A
Comments: The airline was extremely disappointing. I had a connecting flight from Casablanca to Porto with a 5-hour layover, and nearly everyone for that connection was already waiting at the boarding gate. One hour before our scheduled boarding time, we were suddenly informed that the flight, originally scheduled for 5:00 p.m., had already departed at 3:00 p.m. Throughout the wait, I repeatedly asked the airline representative to confirm the gate and showed my boarding pass each time. She assured me that I was at the correct gate and on time. Only later did she inform all of us that we had missed the flight and would need to pay for a new one departing the next day. To make matters worse, the airline did not provide accommodation for the night. This experience was completely unacceptable and reflects a serious lack of responsibility and customer care.

Name: asmac106
Comments: This flight was on time and the aircraft was reasonably clean. Those are the positives. Nonetheless, the cabin crew was the most unprofessional I've ever experienced. They locked up both rear toilets on the plane (both to and from Casablanca) so the red light was on for the entire nine hour flight. The all-male group then used the rear galley as their private party space, talking and laughing for the entire duration of the flight except when they were serving food. They were loud enough to be heard over my noise-cancelling headphones. At Pearson, their flights landed at "the infield," an area far away from both terminals. Passengers had to disembark into a waiting area, wait for a bus, go for a very long ride to the terminal, and then walk to immigration. It was the opposite for the outbound flight. I expect this is cheaper for the airline but not a good experience for tired passengers. There was an extremely bad choice of movies, there were no safety cards, and they ran out of all entrees except vegetarian long before reaching the back of the plane.

Name: GSTTO
Comments: Found a really good deal on lie flat beds to Casablanca with a connection to Marrakech. Same price as Transat Club Class with a stop in Montreal then non-stop to Marrakech but of course no lie flat beds on Transat. The 787-9 were nice as were the seats. The flight crew was pleasant but not very attentive. Food OK. Losing lots of marks on poor processes (such as priority luggage coming last after a 20 minute wait), useless emails, poor signage to their lounge and the list goes on. But it might be worth it for the savings and the ability to get good sleep on board.

Name: Amanda M
Comments: Off to a notable start with RAM after a relative and a friend warned me of the probability of a bad experience. True to form: 2-hour delay, no effort to communicate to the paying public what, where, when, or why of this problem [ no intercom system available in this upgraded airport BTW]. Those with crutches or wheelchairs are ignored. If this waiting continues, we will pick up our car rental by midnight. We were advised to fly via Europe, that even with a lay-over in Europe, from the States, would be far better than any pretention of effort by RAM. Indeed.

Name: Danica A
Comments: If you ever want to experience PTSD and Hell, just fly Royal air maroc. It was the worst and most chaotic experience of my life. Even just to check in bags was a nightmare. That's only the first step. Not only did they disregard our flight times and worked so slow, but they are rude and unwilling to assist. All they do is smirk and make jokes especially the men. After finally checking in our bags after standing in line for 2 hours, we missed our connecting flight. There was no communication and nobody knew basic info or how to solve a simple problem. They gave us wrong directions and sent us around their filthy airport back and forth. On top of that it took 4 hours JUST to get our luggage in baggage claim.. They also leave out your luggage in the open so anyone can easily steal it so good luck trying to locate your luggage. They try to justify it by giving you a hotel (sky hotel and relax hotel) with a food voucher but everything about it was so disgusting. The hotel was atrocious, our room reeked of cigarettes, there was broken glass on the door and their food tasted like punishment. I wouldn't even give a stray dog their food. Our cheesecake had pubic hair and our bedsheets had questionable stains. Probably piss or tears from their prisoners. Prison food looks like Michelin star compared to this hotel. I felt like I was held hostage and trapped. So many people on our flight said it was the worst experience of their life and that they would never fly this airlines again. If I could walk home, I honestly rather would. Save yourself the money and trauma, and never fly royal air maroc.

Name: Randa B
Comments: I would like to express my disappointment with the service provided by the Royal Air Maroc cabin crew during my recent flight. The staff were inattentive and unhelpful, which made the experience unpleasant and far below the level of professionalism expected from the airline. I hope the company takes this feedback seriously to improve the quality of onboard service for future passengers.

Name: Leigh W
Comments: If you have a choice, just dont fly with this company.. Rude staff, and no resolution but just arguing and fighting.. I still cant find out why the boarding stauts on boarding pass is not displayed on the board. Its just absurd, when I tried to check no one is able to tell, but point me to the next place after next places.. No staff is making least effort to help customer.. this is just horrible customer service.

Name: Olu-the-xtian
Comments: I had an extremely distressing experience at Casablanca Airport while flying with Royal Air Maroc. After missing my flight, what should have been a manageable situation turned into a nightmare. **Language Barrier Crisis:** I don’t speak French or Darija (Moroccan Arabic), which created an enormous communication barrier. Getting even basic information across was nearly impossible and deeply frustrating. This was made exponentially worse by the **lack of WiFi at the airport** - I couldn’t even use translation tools on my phone to help bridge the communication gap. **Dismissive and Rude Staff:** The male RAM staff, particularly at the transit desk, were shockingly unhelpful. They were dismissive, rude, visibly angry, and completely unwilling to assist. On multiple occasions, I was outright denied assistance. Female staff members were slightly more helpful but also had limited English proficiency. **Hostile Environment:** Even the airport police were unwelcoming, creating an atmosphere that felt hostile rather than supportive. The overall vibe was “why are you even here?” rather than any attempt to help a stranded passenger. **No Ground Support:** I was ultimately forced to resolve the situation myself by calling RAM customer service on the phone, as they were the only ones who could communicate in English. I had to purchase a new ticket and was stranded at the airport for an extended period, as there’s only one flight per day on this route. **Bottom Line:** If you don’t speak French or Arabic, avoid connecting through Casablanca if at all possible. The absence of WiFi, combined with zero English-speaking support staff and openly hostile treatment, makes this airport exceptionally difficult and stressful for international travelers. Royal Air Maroc’s ground staff need serious training in customer service and basic courtesy. I would not recommend this airport or airline to anyone who doesn’t speak the local languages. -----

Name: Allen S
Comments: The plane did take off and land on time but...the seats were the narrowest I have encountered in decades of travel, for the first time in my life I found the on board restrooms too filthy for comfortable use, the in flight staff was lazy and inattentive, the gate agents at a major US airport did not speak English, check in was chaotic, etc.

Name: David Adeleke
Comments: Very horrible experience. How will they forget my bag and not even care. Very annoying customer service who doesn’t have any empathy

Name: Anne P
Comments: I was apprehensive about flying Royal Air Maroc after reading the reviews, but our flight was fine. Not outstanding but not terrible. The staff was nice and we didn’t have any checked baggage issues. Best tip here was get water before you board - they are a bit stingy with beverages. Also, we put air tags in our luggage, just in case. Didn’t need them for the flights but very helpful for finding your riad when you get lost in the Medina!

Name: Zakaria M
Comments: The mobile app is great and the ticket payment process is smooth. However, some stewards treat customers poorly and their attitude — including their unfriendly smiles — leaves a bad impression.

Name: Gateka N
Comments: It was a horrible experience and the customer service was even worse I lost my suitcase because of them they did not care enough to put the right bag tag(sticker) on my bag and can’t even find it and expect me to be alright with that. My advice is to not use this airline because they are not professionals and they slack a lot

Name: David Antoine F
Comments: The company defrauded me of $2300. The website offeted me to buy an upgrade to business for $1150. I paid once but nothing happened never received any receipt so I assumed it failed and paid again, got a receipt this time. Once at airport they told me there is no available business seat, to go to client service for being reimbursed. Client service refused to proceed, claimed it’s a technical website issue si I should call technical support. Gave up after tens of minutes waiting on the phone. It is plain fraud that the website makes me pay twice for something not available and no human personal physically predent at the airport agreed to reimburse anything.

Name: Dianne D
Comments: Flight to Porto cancelled. Poor communication. Very little help. Put up indirty hotel 35 minutes from airport. Picked up 5am. Had to figure out ourselves how to get boarding pass

Name: Eralp
Comments: They lost my baggage, ignored my messages, and still haven’t responded to my calls about compensation. I’m going to sue them for a substantial amount of money.

Name: Steve P
Comments: Do not fly - pay the extra money to fly another airline. They delayed our initial flight because they lost someone's luggage. We were assured by the flight attendant that our connecting flight would wait - it didn't. 15 people were stranded in Morocco, but they could wait to find someone's luggage. It took 4 hours at the airport to get a new flight which was 3 days later. This travel experience has left a sour memory for a trip that was otherwise beautiful. Royal Air Maroc doesn't care, but they need to keep human impacts of their actions in mind instead of money. I wish nothing but the worst for this airline.

Name: Mal C
Comments: My luggage didn’t arrive at the airport so I filled in the appropriate forms. I was in a different country to where I live. My luggage turned up 6 days later, very badly damaged, both the case and contents. I had had to buy clothes, personal items , make up etc which had cost a lot of money. I applied to Royal Air MAROC for compensation. Their attitude and performance has been TERRIBLE! Emails being sent to them were never answered and then they have the cheek to say I missed the deadline for a claim- which I didn’t! I’m 73 years old, a solo traveller and have travelled the world - but this is the WORST AIRLINE EVER! Fly with another airline

Name: Companion99421
Comments: I’m honestly fed up with this kind of service from Royal Air Maroc. I’ve already sent several complaints, and nothing ever changes. During my recent flight from Casablanca to Guelmim, the ground staff at Mohammed V Airport once again showed a shocking lack of professionalism. The check-in agent refused to process my boarding, asking me to wait for the next team arriving in half an hour — even though her shift was not yet over. She stayed on her phone for about ten minutes, then closed her station and left, leaving me standing right there, completely ignored. When I told her I would file a complaint, she simply shrugged her shoulders, as if it didn’t matter at all. She even sent away every other passenger who came after me, telling them to come back at 18:15 because “your flight is only at 20:00.” I’m a frequent traveller who always chooses my national airline for my international flights, out of loyalty and pride. But honestly, this is the last straw — the drop that made the ocean overflow. One small incident among thousands. Royal Air Maroc represents Morocco. It’s time for things to change — real training, discipline, and respect for passengers should no longer be optional. And honestly, if we’re aiming to host the AFCON or even the World Cup, we’re off to a very bad start — this level of service would be an international embarrassment.

Name: Ndu C
Comments: I traveled from Douala to Paris on the 6th of October 2025 I had 3 baggages right till now non of the bags have not arrived. I have written to them and no thing is being done. All my school things are in the bags. I think it can be better to contact a lawyer. To deal with them.

Name: Rob C
Comments: I arrived in Dakhla 15 days ago and one of my bags didn’t make it. I filed a missing bag report at the airport and was reassured it would arrive the next day. I’m still waiting. Since then, I’ve contacted Royal Air Maroc multiple times – always me calling them, with only two outbound calls from them in over two weeks. Each interaction has been met with indifference and vague responses. The email address listed on their website (where you're told to send your inventory) bounces back – the mailbox is full. That says a lot about how seriously they take lost baggage claims. I’ve repeatedly asked for updates or details on what actions have been taken to locate the bag. The answer is always: “We’ve emailed the airport and are waiting for a response.” When I ask what previous replies have been received, it’s just “It’s a process.” Eventually, I returned to Dakhla airport myself. A helpful RAM staff member there found a bag in the system (with no tag) sitting in Casablanca – very likely mine, based on appearance. Ironically, this was the same untagged bag he flagged on the day I arrived and said would be delivered the next day. He has now re-requested it to be sent to Dakhla, so we’ll see what happens. I also called the RAM customer service line to try and link this untagged bag to the new internal code assigned to it – but they didn’t know what that code was, nor did they show any interest in helping. Just the same line: “We’ll ask the airport.” This is the first time I’ve ever had a bag lost on a flight, and I’m shocked at how poor RAM’s system and customer service have been. My bag is either at Gatwick or Casablanca. I have photos of the bag, a full inventory of its contents – it shouldn’t be this hard for an airline that actually wanted to resolve the issue. Sadly, it feels like they just don’t care.

Name: Jerry D
Comments: I recently flew with Air Maroc for the first time (Economy), and I must admit, I was quite anxious beforehand due to the mixed reviews I’d read online. Thankfully, my experience turned out to be one of the best I’ve had with any airline. From check-in to boarding, inflight service, and disembarkation, everything was smooth and professional. The crew was courteous and attentive, and the overall atmosphere felt welcoming. While the seats showed a bit of wear, it didn’t detract from the comfort or quality of the journey. I’ve already recommended Air Maroc to friends and plan to use them for future trips whenever possible. A big thank you to the hardworking staff who made this flight so enjoyable, you’ve earned my trust and appreciation.

Name: Mr_Caballero
Comments: I booked my flights for November back in March, but two of them (AT 9971 and AT 9970) were cancelled by the company in May. I immediately requested a refund through the official website. Then came the nightmare: months and months with no answer, no help, no money. After nine months of constant emails, endless phone calls, and even visits to several Royal Air Maroc offices in Doha and Spain, I finally managed to get my money back (2634.08 €). It took a ridiculous amount of time and effort to recover something that was rightfully mine. The lack of communication, professionalism and respect towards customers is beyond unacceptable. This has been one of the most frustrating travel experiences I’ve ever had. I will never fly with Royal Air Maroc again, and I strongly advise everyone to think twice before doing so.

Name: Ilya P
Comments: I am writing to formally file a complaint regarding a highly distressing experience I had during my recent travel with Royal Air Maroc. Unfortunately, my checked baggage was lost, and to date, I have not received any clear updates regarding its location or expected return. This situation has caused me significant inconvenience, as the missing bag contained all of my personal clothing and essential items for my trip. I had no alternative clothing or necessities upon arrival, which not only disrupted my travel plans but also led to considerable stress and unexpected expenses. I am extremely disappointed with the lack of communication and support I have received thus far. I expected a higher standard of service from an international airline. I kindly urge you to take immediate action to locate and return my baggage, and to provide appropriate compensation for the losses and inconvenience caused.

Name: vivian a
Comments: This airline is a trap to trap people, to frustrate people to disorganized people this is the worst airline you can ever think about in this 21st century, I traveled with Royal air maroc on July 30th arriving to my destination, which is Lagos on July 31 I traveled from JFK New York Arriving to Lagos. One of my luggage did not arrive and I have to keep calling every single day for my luggage my luggage was not never found up to this day. No word from the company no email no apology. No explanation just utterly silence… I wish I read the reviews before embarking on my journey, and I didn’t even book this ticket. It was an agent that pushed this ticket on me, but it was my worst mistake. My worst travel experience and my worst year ever because of this Airline please if you’re reading this run for your life don’t ever take this Airline. It is a trap!!

Name: najwa b
Comments: I was traveling with Royal Air Maroc yesterday from Tunis to Lisbon, with a short layover in Casablanca (1h20). My first flight (AT 571 at 11:55) was delayed by 1h15, so we arrived in Casablanca at 16:25 and all the connecting flights had already left. It was not only me – all passengers from that flight missed their connections. During the flight, we asked the crew for information about what would happen with our connections, but the stewardesses and even the captain just told us “we don’t have information.” When we arrived in Casablanca, there was no clear assistance. Passengers were running around the airport, not knowing where to go. Finally, when we found a Royal Air Maroc agent, we were simply told we had to wait until the next day for another flight. There were flights available with TAP to Portugal the same evening, but Royal Air Maroc made no effort to rebook us. Our luggage was not returned, so we had nothing with us overnight. The treatment at the hotel was also unacceptable. Non-Arabic passengers received better options, while Arabic passengers were left waiting or told to pay 70 euros at the hotel reception, even when asking for a voucher. The staff showed no respect and offered no fair solution. Overall, this was a terrible experience: missed connections, no communication, no support, and discriminatory treatment. I expected much more professionalism from a national airline.

Name: Lanre M
Comments: Genuinely one of these worst companies I’ve ever had the sheer and utter misfortune of coming across. Not only did they bump us off our flight the day of, 2 days before Christmas, they also tried to feign ignorance on their abhorrent behaviour. After reaching out to complain, that complaint was flat out ignored for months until we initiated legal proceedings. Even then, the customer service has been shocking, and genuinely the worst customer service experience I’ve ever had. Never responding, never courteous, and requiring constant chasers. I do not know what they pay their customer service team but clearly they are paid too much as they don’t seem to want to do their jobs at all. The times I do get through to their phone line, they either don’t give any helpful information at all or they flat out lie

Name: Fady N
Comments: Royal AirMaroc staff broke my daughter’s stroller Uppababy seat, despite it was in a protection bag, to a point that it’s irreparable. It costed me over USD 300 to replace it, yet after 1 month of emails with Royal AirMaroc customer service, they initially offered USD 100 then USD 140, not taking full responsibility for their fault! Very poor customer service from my experience compared to other airlines. I will never book a flight with them again, and will make sure others are aware of my bad experience with them.

Name: Eralp
Comments: I am a Turkish diplomat who came to Bissau from Istanbul via Royal Air Maroc. They lost my luggage and I have not received any response from them for 5 days.

Name: Kehinde K
Comments: Terrible disrespectful officers, rude and unapologetic airline last time flying them. They are slow to do their checks and pay no regard to delay they originally caused missed my flight and the plane was still parked at the gate

Name: Anna P
Comments: I recently traveled on Royal Air Maroc, and my experience was nothing short of a nightmare. My journey began with flight AT514 (07.09.2025, Accra–Casablanca), which was delayed by more than 24 hours due to aircraft issues. This forced me to spend an unexpected night in Accra, made me miss my connecting flight AT960 to Barcelona, and set off a chain of chaos. Upon finally arriving in Casablanca a day late, I was told there were no seats to Barcelona. I requested a reroute on AT968 to Valencia, only to face another 6+ hour delay, which caused me to miss my pre-booked train to Barcelona. With no other choice, I had to change once again to AT964 on 09.09.2025, resulting in a second night in an airport hotel. Even that flight was delayed. In total, I arrived home two days late, exhausted and stressed. To make matters worse, I had no access to my checked luggage throughout this ordeal, meaning I was forced to wear the same clothes for three consecutive days — a situation that was neither acceptable nor humane. Royal Air Maroc completely failed in delivering even the most basic reliability and care. For an airline that presents itself as a national carrier, this experience was beyond unacceptable, and I would strongly advise others to think twice before booking with them.

Name: Adel B
Comments: Bad service and Terrible airline.every time i tried to file a claim through their website i get an error i called them many times to do so they told me to do it on line.i send them an email no answers

Name: Hanae B
Comments: my problem started while coming back to Dubai when the aircraft landed i was travelling with my 9 months old baby i waited as promised by the cabin crew supervisor for the stroller to be brought to the airplane exit i waited and waited and finally i was left unattended with the baby. Wheni approached RAM supervisor she didn't even turned her face toward me she was so rude and ignored my request for help by saying "if don't leave this area i will call you the police" you're delaying my work i don't understand how a airline company hires people like this???we buy the ticket with thousands of money specially to Morocco and then we receive a treat like this..i left crying all alone carrying the baby and hand bags in such big airport asking everyone on my way from airport staff for help they say we cannot leave this zone??? one staff he has some empathy he helped me going by the Metro with the bags till we reached the immigration counter then he called a porter for the rest of the luggage. I always read and hear from people to avoid travelling in RAM i didn't believe it really true plus it was my first time to arrive in DXB airport all my life in UAE i've travelling with Etihad airways From Abu Dhabi.

Name: Wander14016030229
Comments: This was honestly the worst travel experience I’ve ever had. Flights were delayed, connections missed, and instead of helping, Royal Air Maroc staff were rude, dismissive, and uninterested in solving anything. There were earlier flights with available seats, but we weren’t offered them. Staff completely ignored us, more interested in their phones than dealing with stranded passengers. We were eventually dumped in a hotel at 5am without our luggage and told to cover our own transport back to the airport- which they have refused to reimburse!!! To make it worse, duty-free purchases had to be thrown away because we were forced to re-check in. The lack of care, communication, and basic customer service is shocking. If this is how they treat passengers, I can’t imagine how they expect anyone to trust them with long-haul travel. Royal Air Maroc is disorganized, unhelpful, and downright disrespectful to its customers. I would strongly recommend avoiding this airline altogether!!!!

Name: abdelrahmanwael512
Comments: I had a round trip , from Cairo to Sao paulo ( August 9th ) and from Sao paulo to Cairo ( September 2nd ) . I usually don't write reviews but before the flight i read alot of bad reviews on Royal air maroc and i was very scared to fly with them due to the lost luggage issue , but thanks god i didn't have this problem and the service was very good on the plane and luggage arrived on time ( for both me and my friend who was travelling with me ) . I don't know if we were lucky or that ONLY PEOPLE WITH BAD EXPERIENCE MAKE A REVIEW , so i just felt that i need to make this review of the good experience .

Name: Katie F
Comments: Really enjoyed flying with this airline. More leg room than usual short haul providers. Seats more comfortable too. Served a meal and drinks which was included. The plane was however quite dirty and there was no in flight entertainment.

Name: Charles S
Comments: This airline is a joke! Two out of the 4 flights were late and the communication as to why was nonexistent. The only good thing about this airline is the staff.

Name: Florence B
Comments: It’s the most useless international flight I have come across. The staffs seems very racist, they have a different approach with skin colour. I can’t imagine a flight attendant that doesn’t understand English being so rude with a customer that is communicating fluently in English. This flight is rubbish. The whole meal period was absolutely ridiculous with an attitude of just take swallow it monkeys. Such a mini prison flight, if u re looking to discipline a naughty child take them on that flight they will come back and say sorry mum and dad.

Name: Hedy B
Comments: I flew to Morocco via Royal Air Maroc in July and returned in August. I had to change my flight because I wanted to extend my trip. The airline was extrremely accommodating and changed the flight with a low fee. I would recommend this airline to travel from the DC/VA area. It's a direct flight. I did experience a 30-minute delay both ways, but the staff kept us updated while on the plane. Great airline and great staff!!

Name: Voyage35837150567
Comments: If you are considering flying Royal Air Maroc, don’t. This airline is barely operational and absolutely should not be trusted to run on time, let alone deliver people safely from point A to point B. They destroyed a $2,000+ RIMOWA suitcase on the outbound leg to the point of being irreparable, as per Rimowa’s assessment; unsurprisingly, RAM refused to reimburse me in full. On the return, our Marrakech–Casablanca flight was delayed nearly three hours, in 90°F heat, while passengers were confined to a room with no air conditioning and no access to water (except for a vending machine that only accepted euro coins). After waiting, we stood for half an hour to board an airbus that transported us about twenty feet to the plane. I wish I were exaggerating. Ryanair boarded its passengers on their delayed flight before RAM; both were originally set to take off at the same time. In business class on the long-haul to JFK, my seat’s entertainment system was broken; I guess they figured their astounding incompetence as an airline was entertainment enough! RAM’s customer service is equally farcical: stonewalling, insulting offers, and total disregard for international obligations under the Montreal Convention. I travel extensively and have never encountered anything this absurd or hellish.

Name: Moroccan D
Comments: Today I flew with Royal air Maroc from JFK to Casablanca and it was more than and incredible experience. Top notched service from Hanan and her crew who went all their ways to satisfy the guests. One thing that stuck with me, that showed the level of kindness and professionalism is when a passenger was sick the whole crew came together to help her and made sure she was fine. The food was great and thoughtful. The service from the crew was not only professional but also carried with kindness. Great communication was also noticeable. Thank you so much for your efforts and thank you Royal Air Maroc. Abdelhak

Name: Hamza E
Comments: I am a Moroccan living abroad. Before the summer, I booked a round-trip ticket with Royal Air Maroc to spend my holidays in Morocco. Sadly, my father passed away just days before my outbound flight, which forced me to purchase another ticket to attend his funeral. To my shock, on the day of my scheduled return, I discovered that Royal Air Maroc had cancelled my return flight without any refund, claiming it was because I did not take the outbound flight. I received no notification of this cancellation, and it is both unfair and unacceptable that the airline keeps the money for a return ticket that was completely independent of the outbound leg, especially in such a tragic force majeure situation. Each year, His Majesty King Mohammed VI calls for Moroccan expatriates to be welcomed and treated with dignity. Unfortunately, our national airline is still light-years away from providing even a minimally acceptable service.

Name: BigPita
Comments: Everything about this experience was bad. Their online check-in doesn’t work well on mobiles, or their app, couldn’t use a lounge even though I have one world status, the staff were not helpful and decided to throw in additional random checks and the boarding was chaotic. It was just really unpleasant

Name: Heather B
Comments: Booked business class from Milan to NYC with a short connection in Casablanca. When we arrived in Casablanca, we received a message in the app that our seats were transferred to a later flight (15 hours!). We weren’t given a reason why and even though we tried to board our original plane, our tickets were invalid. The airline sent us a 12 other passengers to a nearby hotel for the night. We missed our hotel and dinner reservation in NYC and incurred an expenses staying overnight in Morocco without our luggage. We still have no idea why our tickets were changed when our original flight was on time and we were there to make the connection.

Name: Yves
Comments: Royal Air Maroc: A Disappointing Experience My recent flight with Royal Air Maroc was extremely disappointing. The flight conditions were poor, and the staff's professionalism was lacking. Most concerning was the loss of my luggage and the inadequate follow-up. I had to repeatedly chase for updates, which was incredibly frustrating. I chose this airline due to a lower fare (£1200), but the experience was not worth it. I would not recommend Royal Air Maroc."

Name: Amany B
Comments: Very comfortable, and the crew are very professional, smiling and very helpful. I didn't experienced any delay. The baggage arrived on time.

Name: Zeliboba
Comments: We flew from Moscow via Casablanca to Dulles (Washington). On the first leg the plane was older, on the second leg it was newer, but the cabin was a bit shabby. At the same time, the seats were comfortable, the distances between the rows were comfortable, the food was acceptable. A good selection of films on the second flight, the stewards were attentive. The transfer in Casablanca was well organized, no problems with boarding passes and navigation. The baggage arrived in Washington in full. The only drawback is the organization of check-in at Sheremetyevo Airport. The site did not allow electronic check-in for the entire route (and the site itself does not work very well). And at Sheremetyevo, queue barrier were not made for the check-in queue. As a result, a huge crowd at the start of check-in cannot line up, conflict situations arise, and the employees at the check-in counter do not help in organizing a normal queue.

Name: Tifa A
Comments: It was a horrible experience traveling with RAM delays very bad customer services no one is helping at all, our luggage was lost until now we couldn’t found it still waiting. Staff in airport was very bad. One of them said it’s not his business that we lost our luggage or our flight was delayed.. Food was very very bad we couldn’t eat any of it. This is my last time using Royal air Maroc flight never again..

Name: kravmaguffin
Comments: Easily the worst business class product since Aeroflot or China Eastern. The cabin was filthy...not 'we turned the plane around quickly and didn't clean thoroughly' dirty. No this was 'we truly dislike cleaning products' filthy, with accumulated grime in the seats, damaged panels and a seat cushion that was marginally worse than sleeping on a rock. My back literally hurt after the flight. I cannot imagine what economy was like and based on business, probably skirts a few Geneva convention torture laws. The only reason I didn't give this a one star was the food - surprisingly good.

Name: Navigator597468
Comments: Royal Air Maroc provided the worst customer service experience I’ve ever had—and I am a frequent traveler. On a business class trip from New York to Seville, my luggage and that of my travel companion were lost. My AirTag showed that our bags were sitting in Casablanca, yet despite notifying the airline of their exact location, it still took a full week for them to deliver them. During that time, we had no choice but to spend part of our vacation shopping for clothing and basic necessities. We submitted receipts for these expenses (around €380 each, which was reasonable given the circumstances) to Royal Air Maroc for reimbursement. What followed was months of frustrating delays, repeated requests to resubmit documents, and ultimately, after four months, the airline informed us they would reimburse only €186.35—without offering any explanation for how they reached that figure. This experience showed a complete lack of professionalism and respect for customers, even those traveling in business class. I almost never write reviews, but this ordeal has been so disappointing that I feel compelled to warn others: AVOID ROYAL AIR MAROC AT ALL COSTS

Name: Sam S
Comments: My second leg of the trip from CMN to MCO was cancelled and for 50 days I am unable to get Air Maroc to issue me a refund though I used their website to purchase the ticket. I’ve spoken with too many Air Maroc reps in the USA, including Ikram and Mohammad and they were useless and unhelpful.

Name: chamss l
Comments: Our experience with Royal Air Maroc was extremely disappointing. It began at JFK International Airport, where our flight was delayed four hours due to weather. That was understandable and not an issue. However, the problems began once we landed in Casablanca. We had only 45 minutes to make our connecting flight. At check-in, we were assured we still had time and were allowed to proceed. Yet, when we arrived at the gate, the officers told us the plane had already taken off—this was within only two minutes of our arrival. What made this more concerning was that over 35 other passengers from our same flight also missed that exact connection. It is unreasonable to believe a flight would depart without its connecting passengers, or that no communication was made between the two flights. When we sought assistance, the staff were extremely rude and dismissive. The first employee simply said, “I can’t do anything to help you” and redirected us elsewhere. At the next desk, no one was present. We were sent from one counter to another multiple times, even being directed by a janitor, before finally reaching the agency desk. There, the agent was distracted on the phone with her friends before eventually informing us that the first employee we had spoken to should have resolved the issue. She then cancelled our original tickets, rebooked us for a later flight that evening, and provided a hotel voucher. The hotel was of very poor quality, with bad service and mediocre food. Later that evening, when boarding the bus for our rebooked flight, we were told to get off due to a technical issue. We waited for around three hours with no updates from staff. The only information we received came from a business class passenger who had received an email about the reason for the delay and the new departure time. Eventually, we reached Marrakech and continued our trip. Unfortunately, the problems did not end there. The same agent who rebooked us had also cancelled our return flight on August 20th—without informing us. We only discovered this at the airport on the day of departure, when we were told our names were not in the system. The agency claimed it was our fault for not “confirming,” even though our tickets clearly showed otherwise. As a result, our return flight was pushed back to August 24th. Despite this being entirely Royal Air Maroc’s mistake, they refused to take responsibility or provide proper compensation. On top of all this, the onboard experience was unacceptable. The aircraft itself was very old and unhygienic, the food served was literally inedible, and the flight attendants consistently displayed poor attitudes and rudeness toward passengers. This lack of professionalism, cleanliness, and basic courtesy only added to the overall frustration. After giving Royal Air Maroc many chances over the years, this experience has made it clear that the airline does not value its customers. The lack of communication, accountability, professionalism, and respect is unacceptable. I strongly advise other travelers to avoid Royal Air Maroc and choose an airline that actually cares for its passengers.

Name: Chris
Comments: The food on this flight was terrible to the extent I got food poisoning from the chicken dish and had to be admitted to hospital on 19/8/2025. I told my partner that the chicken tasted old shortly after I ate it. A few hours later I didn't feel well.

Name: K V
Comments: Had a great experience with flying Royal Air Maroc! Had carry-on luggage and backpack with no problems at all. Found the attendants to be super professional, friendly, and caring. Food served on flight was delicious. Passengers were given blankets and pillows for the flight. Smooth flight; can't wait to visit Morocco again!

Name: Lekelefac josel... A
Comments: Worst experience ever. Tried them for the first time and I regret it. Traveled with my parents on the 14th of April 2025, got to Paris and our bags were no where to be found. Till today August 17th 2025, I'm still waiting for my bag. Have done everything they asked for, no refunds , no bag. Some of the bags that came few days later , arrived with spoilt foodstuffs. I will never ever try them again.

Name: arif
Comments: Just got back from a flight from Casablanca. The experience with this airline was a terrible experience. I went to Casablanca with my two elderly parents and young eight year old daughter, I’ve been a few times, but this trip definitely stood out from other trips. First of all their customer service when trying to get a wheel chair for my elderly father was disgusting, the manager in charge simply just ignored us, when I asked about obtaining a wheel chair, I even gave him my boarding pass, complete idiot was pretending he was very busy, when he was just ignoring me, ( huge chip on his shoulder). Took matters into my own hands and had to support my father for a long walk through security and eventually to the gate. After the tiring experience, we began boarding our flight, on the way a bus came to take all the passengers, there was about 50 people on the FIRST bus, including my elderly parents, young daughter and other elderly people, women and children. It was almost 30 degrees in heat, the driver firstly wasn’t driving to the plane, we were all getting very hot, he eventually started driving and then stopped the bus closing all the doors, we were all trapped in this bus and we were screaming at the driver to open the window or door at least, so people could breath, no answer from the driver, he simply ignored us, after asking many times, he refused to answer. We all had to take matters into our own hands and open the doors ourselves. The driver was wearing glasses and a hat, he refused to apologise for his actions. He could have potentially killed people and he had no remorse. Disgusting human being, disgusting customer service.

Name: DrWho43
Comments: Lovely experience. Nice wide seats , excellent service onboard and in check in. Flight was punctual and in flight food was very tasty

Name: moroccan T
Comments: I’ve flown with Royal Air Maroc many times, and I’m writing this review after giving them several chances over the years. Unfortunately, the service hasn’t improved — in fact, it keeps getting more disappointing. The first issue starts the moment you board: the unpleasant smell inside the aircraft makes you question hygiene and cleanliness right away. Once seated, you realize the plane lacks basic amenities — things that should be standard on any flight, like blankets, hygiene kits, or even small towels. I’m referring especially to long-haul flights (5 hours or more), where you expect a minimum level of comfort and professional service. But when you need something, crew members often don’t respond, or they take a long time and come back with excuses like, “We don’t have it.” Even asking for water becomes a hassle — it's brought late, and only in a small plastic cup. If you ask them to refill your personal bottle, you’re told to get up and do it yourself. That is not acceptable for any airline that calls itself “Royal.” On top of that, if you’re asleep during meal service, they won’t save your food or check in with you afterward. If you wake up and request something, they respond in a rude or dismissive way, as if it’s your fault you missed it. There is no sense of customer care or service awareness. To make things worse, the baggage situation is consistently frustrating. Many times, the luggage from different destinations gets mixed up, which causes unnecessary delays. You often find yourself waiting much longer than you should at baggage claim because your suitcase was grouped with bags from another flight. This kind of disorganization shows a lack of efficiency and proper management. We pay high prices to fly with Royal Air Maroc and expect a certain standard in return — at the very least, basic respect and reliable service. Instead, we get told things like, “We have limited stock,” which is not the passenger’s responsibility. If we’re paying for a full service, we deserve one. Honestly, it seems like the level of service changes depending on the flight route. I’ve noticed that flights to the U.S., for example, are treated with more care. So does that mean passengers on other routes are less important? The airline’s name is Royal Air Maroc — and the word “Royal” should mean something. I didn’t write this just out of frustration. I’ve flown with you many times, hoping that someday I’d notice a change, but nothing has improved. It feels like we fly with RAM because we have no other choice — not because the experience is worth it. I truly hope Royal Air Maroc takes this feedback seriously. Learn from other airlines like Qatar Airways, where service is delivered with professionalism and heart. In the aviation industry, you must love what you do — because passengers feel the difference.
